title: Delicate Garlicky Korean Shrimp Tang
description: A light and gooey Korean dish featuring dried shrimp, garlic, and a dash of friendly spices, originating from the Gyeonggi province.
instructions: Rinse the dried shrimp and soak them in water for 30 minutes.
Drain the water and chop the rehydrated shrimp into small pieces.
In a large pan, heat the sesame oil over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the chopped shrimp, soy sauce, gochujang, and fish sauce, and stir to combine.
Cook for 2-3 minutes, until the shrimp is lightly browned.
Add the water to the pan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes, until the sauce has thickened.
Stir in the chopped green onions and toasted sesame seeds.
Serve the tang hot, garnished with additional green onions if desired.
